channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Don’t allow phone searches, FCT CP urges residents, youths
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
FCT Commissioner of Police advises residents, especially youths, not to allow police officers to search their mobile phones during stop-and-search operatio
Read More: https://punchng.com/dont-allow-phone-searches-fct-cp-urges-residents-youths/

item.title: Aiyedatiwa celebrates Akeredolu’s posthumous birthday, vows to sustain ex-governor’s legacy
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ondo State Governor Lucky Aiyedatiwa celebrates Rotimi Akeredolu’s 70th posthumous birthday, vowing to sustain the late ex-governor’s impactful legacy in t

item.description:

item.title: Messi breaks silence after World Cup final loss, congratulates Spain
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lionel Messi speaks out after Argentina’s World Cup final defeat to Spain, expressing pain but pride, and congratulating the champions.
Read More: https://punchng.com/messi-breaks-silence-after-world-cup-final-loss-congratulates-spain/

item.title: ECOWAS reaffirms plan to rollout ECO single currency in 2027
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
ECOWAS reaffirms its commitment to launching the ECO single currency in 2027, aiming for deeper economic integration and sustainable growth in West Africa.
Read More: https://punchng.com/ecowas-reaffirms-plan-to-rollout-eco-single-currency-in-2027/

item.title: Fire razes 17 shops in Lagos market
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
An early morning Lagos market fire in Ikorodu’s Sabo Market destroyed 17 shops. Learn about the cause and emergency response efforts.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fire-razes-17-shops-in-lagos-market/

item.title: Police Launch Skills Acquisition Scheme for Junior Officers’ Spouses
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Inspector-General of Police, IGP Olatunji Rilwan Disu, has joined the Nigeria Police Trust Fund (NPTF) and the Police Officers’ Wives Association (POWA) to launch a skills acquisition and empowerment programme for spouses of junior police officers. The maiden initiative was unveiled at the MD Ab

item.title: Edo Gov’t Suspends Commissioner, Another Official Over Alleged Official Infraction
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Edo State Government has suspended the Commissioner for Livestock Development, Prof. Omorodion Ignatius Ikponmwosa, and the State Project Coordinator of the Livestock Productivity and Resilience Support (LPRES) Project, Mrs. Ikpikhumi Betsy Aghaku, over an alleged official infraction. The govern

item.title: Police exhume body of man beaten to death, arrest four in Akwa Ibom
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Akwa Ibom police exhume the body of a man beaten to death and arrest four suspects. Discover details of the alleged murder and ongoing investigation.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-exhume-body-of-man-beaten-to-death-arrest-four-in-akwa-ibom/

item.title: Sahara Group Foundation Expands Recycling Hub In Kaduna, Jigawa States
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Sahara Group Foundation has commissioned two new Sahara Go Recycling Hubs in Jigawa and Kaduna States, extending structured waste management and economic opportunity to communities in Northern Nigeria. The hubs, at Gidan Hakimi, Shuwarin Local Government Area, Jigawa State, and Asharami Retail Stati

item.title: Ex-England Captain And Manager Kevin Keegan Dies After Battling Cancer
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: England national team captain and manager, Kevin Keegan, has died at age 75 following a long battle with abdominal cancer. His family confirmed his death in a statement on Monday, stating that he was surrounded by wife and daughters during his final moments. “It is with immense sadness that we annou

item.description: Bournemouth midfielder Alex Scott rejects a new contract amid growing Premier League interest in the England international.

The arrival of the trio  increased the number of  players in camp to 22 as the Super Falcons intensify preparations ahead of the competition. Three more players includi

item.title: Federal High Court stops FCCPC from issuing licences
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Federal High Court in Lagos on Monday held that Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission, FCCPC, has no authority to issues licences but can only oversee airtime and data credit services. The court clarified that the Commission’s powers function alongside those of t

item.title: Isaac Fayose says Ayo Fayose deserves ministerial role, not REA chairmanship
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Isaac Fayose has said his elder brother, former Ekiti State Governor Ayo Fayose, deserves a more prominent federal appointment than the newly announced chairmanship of the Rural Electrification Agency (REA). The post Isaac Fayose says Ayo Fayose deserves ministerial role, not REA chairmanship appear

item.title: Nicaragua President, Ortega bans elections after decades in power
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nicaragua’s President, Daniel Ortega has ruled out future elections, announcing that he will work with the National Assembly to pass new laws that will “build a wall” against the opposition. In a speech marking the 1979 Sandinista revolution, in which Ortega helped overthrow the An

item.description: Tottenham have signed France international Alice Sombath for a club record fee from Lyon.

item.title: Samsung Launches First US Credit Card With Barclays, Offers Up to 5% Cash Rewards
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Samsung has unveiled its first US credit card with Barclays, giving customers up to 5% cash rewards on Samsung purchases, Samsung Wallet spending and everyday transactions with no annual fee. But as Nigeria’s economic terrain evolves, this

item.title: Morocco: West African Leaders Back $25b Nigeria-Morocco Gas Pipeline
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Daba Finance] West African leaders have endorsed the Nigeria-Morocco Gas Pipeline, giving political backing to a $25 billion project designed to transport natural gas across West Africa and into Europe as the continent looks to strengthen energy security and expand regional integration.

item.title: Crisis Rocks Niger APC As Ex-Governor’s Aide Warns Against Looming Mass Defection
item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.title: Cross River residents fall prey to another Ponzi scheme, leaving hundreds in Tears
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Angry clients stormed the company’s office on Friday to discover that operators had fled, prompting them to cart away available items left behind, including 10-kilogramme bags of rice, office furniture and bicycles. item.title: Jamie Dimon says he wouldn’t buy Treasurys. ‘I don’t understand the upside.’
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Jamie Dimon — long considered a potential candidate to be treasury secretary, and the person in charge of one of the nation’s primary dealers of Treasury securities — says he wouldn’t buy American debt.

item.description: The Gbenga Hashim Solidarity Movement (GHSM) has cautioned the Osun State Governor, Ademola Adeleke against actions, it said could deepen divisions within the Accord Party, warning that internal wrangling would undermine the party’s fortunes ahead of the 2027 general election. item.description: Dutch model and influencer Jelicia Westhoff accused Super Eagles goalkeeper Maduka Okoye of physical assault, routine infidelity, and child neglect. The sudden development unfolded shortly after Okoye was spotted in Venice, Italy, alongside American rapper Cardi B at the Jean Paul Gaultier Fall/Wint

item.title: Why Super Falcons Can Successfully Defend WAFCON Title —Nwadike
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Former Nigeria international Rita Nwadike has backed the Super Falcons to retain their Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) at this year’s tournament in Morocco. Nwadike, who was a member of the Super Falcons squad to the 1995 and 1999 women’s World Cup, expressed confidence in the quality and exp

item.title: 143 Companies Vie for 50 Oil, Gas Blocks At NUPRC Commercial Bid Conference
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A total of 143 pre-qualified companies will converge on Abuja on July 21 to compete for 50 oil and gas blocks in the Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission’s (NUPRC) Commercial Bid Conference. The conference, set for Abuja, follows a rigorous prequalification process for the 2025 Licens

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: A United States court has sentenced notorious Mexican drug lord Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada to life imprisonment, bringing an end to the decades-long criminal career of one of the world's most powerful narcotics traffickers. item.description: Rabiu Kwankwaso, running mate to the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) presidential candidate, Peter Obi, has said their administration would engage terrorists and other criminal elements in dialogue as part of efforts to end insecurity if elected in the 2027 presidential election. item.title: Beijing summons Philippine ambassador over clash in disputed South China Sea shoal
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Beijing and Manila summoned each other's ambassadors Tuesday following a confrontation between Chinese and Philippine sailors at a disputed shoal in the South China Sea. China continues to claim sovereignty over almost the entirety of the global trade route.

item.title: Gilts Steady as Investors Await More Policy Details From Burnham
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: UK government bonds posted small moves as investors awaited fresh policy details from new Prime Minister Andy Burnham, while weak economic data dimmed bets on higher interest rates.

item.description: The Bauchi State Police Command has arrested a 40-year-old man, Clement Anthony, in connection with the killing of a community leader in Bauchi metropolis and recovered the firearm allegedly used in the attack. item.title: US Launches Fresh Strikes On Iran As Conflict Escalates
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The United States has carried out a fresh wave of airstrikes against Iran, marking the 10th consecutive night of military action, as President Donald Trump vowed retaliation for the deaths of three American service members. The post US Launches Fresh Strikes On Iran As Conflict Escalates appeared fi

item.title: Two die as Lexus car collides with motorcycle in Abeokuta
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Two men have been confirmed dead in a fatal road crash that occurred on Monday, at Olorunsogo, Leme area of the Abeokuta South Local Government, Ogun State.   The crash happened at about 5:45 p.m, involving a black Lexus car with registration number KJA 398 JR and a black TVS motorcyc

item.title: Flutterwave’s Fidelis Chiwara advocates stronger ecosystem collaboration at AT50 Market Open
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Fidelis Chiwara, Global Expansions and Payment Partnerships Lead for Southern Africa at Flutterwave, joined business leaders and investors at the Johannesburg Stock Exchange (JSE) for the Africa Tech 50 (AT50) Market Open panel discussion on the future of Africa’s leading private companies. Speaking

item.title: FULL LIST: ECOWAS approves appointment of new officials
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) has approved the appointment of new persons to The post FULL LIST: ECOWAS approves appointment of new officials appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: FG debunks N80tn borrowing claims, blames debt surge on naira devaluation
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: …Says debt figures inflated by exchange rate revaluation, Ways and Means securitisation …Senators raise concerns over slow implementation of 2026 read more FG debunks N80tn borrowing claims, blames debt surge on naira devaluation

item.title: Police Arrest Suspect Over Killing Of Community Leader In Bauchi
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Bauchi State Police Command has arrested a 40-year-old man, Clement Anthony, over the alleged killing of the Mai Unguwar Ngas, Jacob Bature, during a shooting incident in Bauchi metropolis. Police spokesperson, SP Nafiu Habib, said the incident occurred on July 19, 2026, at Unguwar Ngas, Inkil,

item.title: Boko haram terrorists move families around Lake Chad amid military onslaught [Video]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A video purportedly showing suspected Boko Haram members travelling with their wives and children around the Lake Chad area has surfaced online. An X user, The Crusader, who shared a video on Tuesday, claimed the terrorists were relocating to a new camp amid intensified military operations

item.title: FirstHoldCo Posts Record H1 PIT 83.5% YoY Increase to N653.5 billion
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Nigerian financial services group FirstHoldCo Plc reported a record first-half performance on Monday, with profit before tax rising 83.5% year-on-year to 653.5 billion naira, driven by stronger operating income, growth in non-interest revenue and improved balance sheet quality following a strategic

item.title: Buba Galadima ruined Kwankwaso’s political fortunes – Bature
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Director-General, Media and Communications to Kano State Governor, Abba Kabir Yusuf, Sunusi Bature Dawakin Tofa, has launched a a verbal attack on Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) chieftain, Engr. Buba Galadima, describing him as a political opportunist who has contributed nothing to the growth

item.title: Experts task African countries on housing for low-income earners, informal workers
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Stakeholders across Africa have tasked governments on the need to tackle housing crisis among the low-income and informal workers across the continent. While suggesting solutions to the housing crisis, the stakeholders urged Africa governments to prioritise housing for the low – income group,

item.title: London Stock Exchange to Introduce Nonstop Trading Next Year
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The London Stock Exchange will open a new venue outside of its regular market hours, a move to offer “near-continuous trading” and better compete with the 24/7 trading espoused by crypto platforms.

item.title: Northern Christians question N1.9bn church allocation in N8bn religious budget
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Northern Christian Association (NCA) has called on the Federal Government to explain what it described as a significant disparity in the allocation of about N8.05 billion for church and mosque projects in the proposed 2026 Appropriation Bill, saying the distribution raises concerns about fairnes

item.title: EFCC clears Immigration boss Kemi Nandap from ongoing visa fraud investigations
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has cleared the Comptroller General of the Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS), Kemi Nandap, of any involvement in its ongoing visa fraud investigations. The post EFCC clears Immigration boss Kemi Nandap from ongoing visa fraud investigations appeare

item.title: ECOWAS names new commissioners, judges for 2026-2030 tenure
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “The Authority endorses the recommendations from the Ninety-Sixth Ordinary Session of the Council of Ministers relating to statutory appointments to positions in Community Institutions for the period 2026–2030,” the communique said.

item.title: Chinese Consul General Commends Grace Schools For Promoting Chinese Language, Culture
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Chinese Consul General in Lagos, Ms. Yan Yuqing, has commended Grace Schools, Gbagada, Lagos, for integrating Chinese language and culture into its curriculum, describing the initiative as a boost to bilateral relations between Nigeria and China. Yuqing gave the commendation during a visit to th
